title: IIOS Audit Event
description: One entry in the append-only, hash-chained Audit log. See docs/25_AUDIT_EVENT_MODEL.md for the field-required-by-event_type table this schema enforces structurally for the most sensitive event types.

actor.description: actor_id that caused the event. For system-generated events (e.g. governance_unavailable) use 'system:governance'.

evidence.description: Pointers only (e.g. object storage keys, decision_id). Never raw secret values.

previous_event_hash.description: SHA-256 of the previous event in this chain. '0'*64 for the chain's genesis event.
previous_event_hash.$comment: previous_event_hash IS included in the material hashed to produce this event's own event_hash. This schema fixes the field's shape only; it does not and cannot enforce the hashing procedure. See docs/25_AUDIT_EVENT_MODEL.md for the recommended canonicalization (RFC 8785 JCS, not implemented in this change) and full chain-verification procedure.

event_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical serialization of every other field in this event, including previous_event_hash but excluding event_hash itself.
event_hash.$comment: event_hash cannot be part of its own input. 'Canonical serialization' means RFC 8785 (JSON Canonicalization Scheme), recommended in docs/25_AUDIT_EVENT_MODEL.md but not implemented here. This field's regex pattern only validates that a value looks like a lowercase hex SHA-256 digest; it cannot validate that the digest was computed correctly, that a JCS-conformant serializer was used, or that the chain is untampered — those require the verification procedure in docs/25_AUDIT_EVENT_MODEL.md, run outside JSON Schema.
